item.title: Scaling Terraform Across many Teams: A Native Framework for Platform Engineering
item.description: TL;DR: A pure Terraform framework that lets 50+ teams self-service infrastructure by...

item.title: How We Build Software with AI Assistants
item.description: From brainstorm to production in four phases — structured AI collaboration that produces better software than either alone.
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: Making SQE Work Everywhere: Pluggable Auth and Catalogs
item.description: How we're turning a single-vendor query engine into something that runs against any identity provider, any catalog, and any cloud.
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: When Your SQL Engine Understands Meaning
item.description: SQL engines know table shapes. We're adding ontologies, property graphs, vector search, and AI-native interfaces.
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: We Replaced Our Trino Fork with a Rust SQL Engine
item.description: How we went from maintaining a 2M-line Java fork to shipping a 50MB binary that runs every query as the authenticated user.
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: Building a Comprehensive SQL Benchmark Suite
item.description: Seven benchmark suites, 222 queries, and the infrastructure to measure performance honestly.
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: Bringing dbt to the Data Platform: A Browser-Based IDE
item.description: Integrating dbt Core into Chameleon with a full workspace IDE, git operations, lineage visualization, and AI-assisted model development.
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: AI Flows: Langflow as the Platform's Intelligence Layer
item.description: Embedding Langflow as a managed AI service with auto-switching flows, MCP tool integration, and context-aware assistance.
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: A Visual Editor for Open Data Contracts
item.description: A full ODCS v3.1.0 contract editor with tabbed forms, live YAML preview, catalog import with AI-suggested quality checks, and dbt export.
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: Docker Dev Mode: Hot Reload for the Full Stack
item.description: A docker-compose.dev.yml overlay for instant feedback on frontend and backend changes without rebuilding containers.
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: Databricks-Style Column Profiling in the Data Explorer
item.description: Inline column statistics with distribution sparklines computed from real SQL queries, not just Iceberg manifest metadata.
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: Dropping Radix UI: Going Fully Custom for React 19
item.description: How a React 19 infinite re-render loop led us to replace all third-party UI primitives with 660 lines of custom components.
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: From 63% to 95%: Building Trino SQL Compatibility in a Single Day
item.description: Implementing 70+ UDFs, Iceberg time travel, metadata TVFs, and engine-level SQL features for Trino drop-in replacement.
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: Streaming Writes, Sort Order Safety, and the IN (Subquery) Workaround
item.description: Fixing OOM in CTAS, safe Iceberg sort order for mixed writers, and working around DataFusion limitations.
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: Five Layers of Caching and an 8.8x Speedup Over Trino
item.description: How multi-layer caching took SQE from slower than Trino to 2.5-8.8x faster across every benchmark suite.
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: How Agentic AI Helped Us Beat Trino
item.description: 221 queries, 7 suites, one week — how an AI assistant running automated benchmarks drove a major performance breakthrough.
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: Auditing a Data Platform with AI: Iterative Security Hardening
item.description: Four audit rounds with escalating AI personas found 42 security issues and removed 6,500 lines of dead code.
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: When the Ground Shifts Faster Than People Can Stand
item.description: A companion piece to The Human Side of the Machine Shift — exploring why AI disruption hits different minds differently, and how leadership can turn discomfort into team strength.
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: Re:invent 2023 News
item.description: reinvent 23 news
item.author: Jacob Verhoeks

item.title: The Database Evolution: Breaking Free from Monolithic Thinking
item.description: A technical analysis of AWS's latest database innovations, from Aurora's Graviton4 support to OpenSearch Serverless improvements. Learn how these changes are reshaping data architecture patterns and enabling more efficient, distributed database systems with real-world implementation strategies.
item.author: Cloud Architect's Perspective

item.title: The Silent Revolution in Disaster Recovery: How AWS is Changing the Game
item.description: Explore how AWS's new zonal shift capabilities and Security Incident Response features are transforming traditional disaster recovery. This technical deep-dive reveals how automatic failover, self-healing systems, and integrated security responses are making DR more reliable and cost-effective than ever before.
item.author: Cloud Architect's Perspective

item.title: The Human Touch in Digital Transformation: Beyond Just Technology
item.description: An architect's perspective on AWS's latest customer experience innovations, including Amazon Connect's AI capabilities and Polly's new synthetic voices. Discover how these technologies are making digital interactions more natural while improving efficiency and customer satisfaction.
item.author: Cloud Architect's Perspective

item.title: Infrastructure Efficiency: The Hidden Environmental Impact of AWS's Latest Announcements
item.description: Discover how AWS's latest infrastructure updates, particularly the expansion of Graviton4 processors and R8g instances, are quietly revolutionizing cloud computing sustainability. Learn about real-world performance improvements, cost reductions, and environmental benefits of these strategic changes.
item.author: Cloud Architect's Perspective
